Details: Removal of mercury by conventional treatment processes can be very effective (up to 90 percent) under appropriate treatment conditions. All data collected were performed on the bench-scale level.  Conventional treatment has been identified by the EPA as a Best Available Technology (BAT) for mercury removal. Based on the findings of one study, alum and ferric coagulants performed similarly for mercury removal. The membrane filters used in the bench-scale experiments were also shown to remove mercury depending on the nature of the membrane.
